subject: Computer Networking
title: Digital Signatures
description: A micro-lesson that makes Digital Signatures usable.

hook.text: How do you prove an email really came from the sender? A digital signature makes forgery mathematically impossible.

buildup.text: The sender hashes the message and encrypts the hash with their private key. That encrypted hash is the signature.

discovery.text: Anyone with the sender's public key can decrypt the signature and compare it to their own hash of the message.

twist.text: If even one character changed in transit, the hashes won't match. Tampering is instantly detected.

climax.text: Software updates, legal documents, and TLS certificates all use digital signatures to prove authenticity.

punchline.text: A digital signature proves who sent it and that nothing changed.

quiz.question: What does a digital signature prove?
options: The sender's identity and that the message wasn't altered
That the message was encrypted during transmission
That the recipient has read the message
